Synthetic source-check fixture

Provenance: Original test text written for the Lenses repository. It does not
quote or adapt a published article and refers to no real event or person.

The Northbridge Observatory reported that three monitoring stations recorded a
short burst of unusual light shortly after midnight. The observatory said that
the stations agreed on the timing but not on the apparent direction.

A municipal notice attributed the difference to maintenance work on one sensor.
An independent astronomy club said the available readings were not sufficient
to distinguish a sensor fault from an atmospheric effect. Neither source
reported injuries, service interruptions, or a continuing hazard.

The observatory plans to publish calibrated readings on Friday. Until those
readings are available, the cause remains unresolved.
